instruments.label: EU Artificial Intelligence Act
instruments.description: The European Union's binding legal framework for AI, including obligations and enforcement arrangements for providers of general-purpose AI models and models with systemic risk.

instruments.label: EU General-Purpose AI Code of Practice
instruments.description: A voluntary compliance tool intended to help providers demonstrate compliance with AI Act obligations concerning transparency, copyright, and safety and security.
instruments.notes: The Code is not treated as equivalent to, or as the source of, the underlying binding obligations in the AI Act.

instruments.label: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
instruments.description: A voluntary framework for organizations to manage risks arising from the design, development, deployment, and use of AI systems.
instruments.notes: It is not counted as binding regulatory authority.

instruments.label: UK AI Security Institute evaluation programme
instruments.description: A government technical programme for developing and conducting evaluations of advanced AI capabilities, safeguards, and impacts.
instruments.notes: Evaluation capacity is distinguished from compulsory regulatory or enforcement power.

instruments.label: China’s Interim Measures for Generative Artificial Intelligence Services
instruments.description: Binding interim administrative measures governing the provision of generative AI services to the public in China.

instruments.label: OECD AI Principles and Policy Navigator
instruments.description: Non-binding AI principles and a comparative policy resource used to understand national policy and institutional approaches.
instruments.notes: It is not counted as enforcement authority.
